Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Let me start by recalling the standard P2P sequence: it should follow requisition approval purchase order goods receipt invoice payment closure.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Looking at Case 1001, the sequence seems correct and follows the ideal flow. No issues there.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1002 immediately stands out because the purchase order was issued before the requisition was approved. Thats a clear violation of controls. Also, payment was released before goods receipt and even before approval, which is highly risky.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1003 has payment released before goods receipt and invoice matching. Thats problematic because payment should only happen after verifying goods and matching invoices.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1004 shows goods received before the purchase order was issued, which breaks the fundamental principle that a PO must exist before procurement.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: I should explain each anomaly clearly, linking them to business risks like fraud, financial loss, or compliance issues. The user likely wants to understand not just whats wrong but why it matters in practice. Ill structure the response by case, highlighting the deviations and their implications.
